@@ -23,7 +23,7 @@
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.5.1","title":"Suggestions: data model + registry keyed by rule id","description":"## Objective

Define the suggestion data model and implement a registry lookup by stable rule id.

## Background

When DCG blocks a command, users need actionable guidance:
- What safer alternatives exist?
- How can they preview the effect first?
- How can they allowlist if intentional?

A well-designed suggestion system improves UX and reduces frustration.

## SuggestionKind Enum

```rust
/// Type of suggestion to help the user
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
#[serde(rename_all = "snake_case")]
pub enum SuggestionKind {
    /// "Run this first to preview the effect"
    /// e.g., "Run `git diff` before `git reset --hard`"
    PreviewFirst,

    /// "Use this safer alternative instead"
    /// e.g., "Use `git reset --soft` or `--mixed` instead of `--hard`"
    SaferAlternative,

    /// "Fix your workflow to avoid this situation"
    /// e.g., "Commit your changes before resetting"
    WorkflowFix,

    /// "Read the documentation for more context"
    /// e.g., "See: https://git-scm.com/docs/git-reset"
    Documentation,

    /// "How to allowlist this specific rule"
    /// e.g., "To allow: `dcg allow core.git:reset-hard --reason '...'`"
    AllowSafely,
}
```

## Suggestion Struct

```rust
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct Suggestion {
    /// Type of suggestion
    pub kind: SuggestionKind,

    /// Human-readable suggestion text
    pub text: String,

    /// Optional command the user can copy/paste
    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    pub command: Option<String>,

    /// Optional URL for documentation
    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    pub url: Option<String>,
}
```

## Registry Implementation

```rust
use std::sync::LazyLock;
use std::collections::HashMap;

/// Registry of suggestions keyed by rule_id (e.g., "core.git:reset-hard")
static SUGGESTION_REGISTRY: LazyLock<HashMap<&'static str, Vec<Suggestion>>> =
    LazyLock::new(|| {
        let mut m = HashMap::new();

        m.insert("core.git:reset-hard", vec![
            Suggestion {
                kind: SuggestionKind::PreviewFirst,
                text: "Run `git diff` and `git status` to see what would be lost".into(),
                command: Some("git diff && git status".into()),
                url: None,
            },
            Suggestion {
                kind: SuggestionKind::SaferAlternative,
                text: "Use `git reset --soft` or `--mixed` to preserve changes".into(),
                command: Some("git reset --soft HEAD~1".into()),
                url: None,
            },
            Suggestion {
                kind: SuggestionKind::WorkflowFix,
                text: "Consider using `git stash` to save changes temporarily".into(),
                command: Some("git stash".into()),
                url: None,
            },
        ]);

        // ... more rules
        m
    });

/// Look up suggestions for a rule
pub fn get_suggestions(rule_id: &str) -> Option<&'static [Suggestion]> {
    SUGGESTION_REGISTRY.get(rule_id).map(|v| v.as_slice())
}
```

## Output Formatting

### Hook Deny Output (stderr)
Show only top 1-3 suggestions to keep output bounded:

```
╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
║  ⚠️  BLOCKED: git reset --hard HEAD~5                        ║
╠══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣
║  Rule: core.git:reset-hard                                   ║
║  Reason: Hard reset can permanently lose uncommitted changes ║
╠══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣
║  💡 Try instead:                                             ║
║  • Run `git diff` first to see what would be lost            ║
║  • Use `git reset --soft` to preserve changes                ║
╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝
```

### Explain Mode (JSON)
Include all suggestions in structured format:

```json
{
  "decision": "deny",
  "rule_id": "core.git:reset-hard",
  "reason": "Hard reset can permanently lose uncommitted changes",
  "suggestions": [
    {
      "kind": "preview_first",
      "text": "Run `git diff` and `git status` to see what would be lost",
      "command": "git diff && git status"
    },
    {
      "kind": "safer_alternative",
      "text": "Use `git reset --soft` or `--mixed` to preserve changes",
      "command": "git reset --soft HEAD~1"
    }
  ]
}
```

## Requirements

- Registry lookup is O(1) via HashMap
- Zero allocations on lookup (return static references)
- Suggestions are stable and deterministic (iteration order preserved)
- JSON output uses snake_case for kind values

## Acceptance Criteria

- Registry lookup is fast and allocation-light.
- JSON output for explain includes suggestions in a stable order.
- All SuggestionKind variants are used appropriately across rules.

## Tests

- Unit tests for registry lookup and ordering.
- Snapshot tests for JSON output format.
- Coverage test: assert all core pack rules have at least one suggestion.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:06:31.606222639-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-08T10:31:43.691276501-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-08T10:31:43.691276501-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented SuggestionKind enum, Suggestion struct, SUGGESTION_REGISTRY, get_suggestions(), 11 unit tests. Core git/filesystem rules populated.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.5.1","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:07:01.11809851-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.5.1","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-99e.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:22:31.439334729-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.5.2","title":"Suggestions: populate core.git + core.filesystem","description":"## Objective

Populate suggestions for the highest-traffic and highest-impact rules:

- `core.git:*`
- `core.filesystem:*`

## What to include

For each rule:

- a short "preview first" command when applicable
- a safer alternative when one exists
- the recommended allowlist command (rule-id based)

Examples (conceptual):

- `core.git:reset-hard`:
  - suggest `git stash` / `git diff` / `git reset --soft|--mixed`
- `core.git:push-force-*`:
  - suggest `--force-with-lease`
- `core.filesystem:rm-rf-*`:
  - suggest verifying path and using safer deletion flows when possible

## Acceptance criteria

- Every core rule has at least one suggestion.
- Suggestions are accurate and generic (no environment assumptions).

## Tests

- A "coverage" test that asserts all core destructive patterns have at least 1 suggestion.

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.5.3","title":"Suggestions: populate high-signal non-core packs (docker/k8s/db)","description":"## Objective

Populate suggestions for the packs most likely to appear in real dev workflows:

- Docker
- kubectl
- databases (psql/mysql/redis)

## Guidance

Prefer "preview" suggestions:

- docker: `docker system df` before `docker system prune`
- kubectl: `--dry-run=client` / `kubectl diff`
- SQL: backup / transaction guidance (keep generic)

## Acceptance criteria

- High-signal destructive patterns in these packs have suggestions.
- Suggestions are concise and safe.
","notes":"Agent Opus starting work: will add docker/kubectl/database suggestions to suggestions.rs","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:06:41.789029161-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-09T01:45:01.323813985-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-09T01:45:01.323813985-05:00","close_reason":"Added Docker (9), Kubernetes (11), and database (22) suggestions following preview-first pattern","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.5.3","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:07:11.302023318-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.5.3","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:22:41.631324095-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.5.4","title":"Integrate suggestions into deny output and explain mode","description":"## Objective

Make suggestions visible where they matter:

- hook deny output (stderr) includes the top suggestion(s)
- `dcg explain` includes all suggestions (pretty/json/compact)

This task is also where we implement the **guided learning workflow** (hook-safe, non-interactive):

- on block, print copy/pasteable next steps (`dcg explain ...`, `dcg allowlist add ...`)
- no prompts; the hook must never wait for user input

## Requirements

### Hook deny output

- Must remain bounded; show only top 1–3 suggestions.
- Must include:
  - stable rule id (`pack_id:pattern_name`)
  - a short reason
  - at least one safe alternative suggestion (when available)
  - a copy/paste allowlist command example, e.g.:
    - `dcg allowlist add <rule_id> --reason "..." --project`
  - a copy/paste explain command:
    - `dcg explain "<command>"`

### Explain output

- Show all suggestions.
- JSON includes suggestions as structured objects.

## Acceptance criteria

- Deny output shows stable rule id + at least one suggestion.
- Deny output includes copy/paste learning commands (explain + allowlist add).
- Explain mode shows suggestions in all formats.

## Tests

- Snapshot tests for deny output (stderr) for a few core rules.
- Explain output tests include suggestions list.
- Regression test: deny output stays under a size cap even if a rule has many suggestions.
","notes":"## Review Additions (2026-01-08)

### Determinism
- Suggestion ordering must be deterministic:
  - sort by severity then stable rule ID
  - cap list with a stable tie-breaker
- Output formatting must not depend on hash iteration order.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:06:46.881705224-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-09T02:41:11.984179419-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.5.4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:07:16.392198141-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.5.4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:22:46.724344957-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.5.4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:22:51.819635023-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.5.4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:22:56.914257712-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.5.4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:23:02.00798218-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.5.5","title":"Suggestions: correctness and coverage tests","description":"## Objective

Prevent suggestion drift and ensure suggestions remain helpful.

## Tests

- Coverage:
  - assert that all patterns with `pattern_name` in core packs have at least one suggestion
- Correctness (spot checks):
  - ensure suggestions never include explicitly forbidden destructive commands (e.g., do not suggest `rm -rf`)
- Ordering:
  - stable output ordering across runs

## Acceptance criteria

- Suggestions are maintained as a first-class UX surface and do not regress silently.

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.6","title":"Implement dcg explain CLI subcommand","description":"## Objective

Implement the `dcg explain <command>` CLI subcommand that provides a full decision trace for any command.

## Background

When DCG blocks (or allows) a command, users and developers need to understand WHY:
- Which patterns matched?
- Which packs were evaluated?
- Was it blocked by a destructive pattern or allowed by a safe pattern?
- What context classification was applied?

The explain mode provides complete transparency into DCG's decision-making process.

## CLI Interface

```bash
# Basic usage
dcg explain "git reset --hard HEAD~5"

# Output format options
dcg explain "command" --format pretty   # Human-readable (default)
dcg explain "command" --format json     # Machine-readable
dcg explain "command" --format compact  # Single-line summary

# Verbose mode (include all evaluated packs, not just matching)
dcg explain "command" --verbose
```

## Decision Trace Structure

The explain output should show the complete decision pipeline:

```
╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
║  DCG Decision Trace                                              ║
╠══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣
║  Input: git reset --hard HEAD~5                                  ║
╠══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣
║  1. Parse                                                        ║
║     └─ Tool: Bash                                                ║
║     └─ Command: git reset --hard HEAD~5                          ║
╠──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╣
║  2. Quick Reject                                                 ║
║     └─ Keywords found: git                                       ║
║     └─ Result: CONTINUE (keywords present)                       ║
╠──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╣
║  3. Normalization                                                ║
║     └─ Original: git reset --hard HEAD~5                         ║
║     └─ Normalized: git reset --hard HEAD~5                       ║
╠──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╣
║  4. Context Classification                                       ║
║     └─ Span[0..25]: Executed                                     ║
║     └─ No safe-string-arg context detected                       ║
╠──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╣
║  5. Heredoc Detection                                            ║
║     └─ Tier 1 trigger: NO                                        ║
║     └─ Result: SKIP heredoc analysis                             ║
╠──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╣
║  6. Pack Evaluation                                              ║
║     └─ Enabled packs: core.git, core.filesystem                  ║
║     └─ Pack: core.git                                            ║
║        ├─ Safe pattern check: NO MATCH                           ║
║        └─ Destructive pattern check:                             ║
║           └─ MATCH: reset-hard                                   ║
║              Pattern: git reset --hard                           ║
║              Reason: Hard reset can permanently lose commits     ║
╠──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╣
║  7. Decision                                                     ║
║     └─ Result: DENY                                              ║
║     └─ Rule ID: core.git:reset-hard                              ║
║     └─ Reason: Hard reset can permanently lose commits           ║
╠──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╣
║  8. Suggestions                                                  ║
║     └─ Run `git diff` first to see what would be lost            ║
║     └─ Use `git reset --soft` to preserve changes                ║
║     └─ To allow: dcg allow core.git:reset-hard --reason "..."    ║
╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝
```

## JSON Output Format

```json
{
  "input": {
    "raw": "git reset --hard HEAD~5",
    "tool": "Bash"
  },
  "trace": {
    "quick_reject": {
      "keywords_found": ["git"],
      "result": "continue"
    },
    "normalization": {
      "original": "git reset --hard HEAD~5",
      "normalized": "git reset --hard HEAD~5"
    },
    "context_classification": {
      "spans": [
        {"range": [0, 25], "kind": "executed"}
      ]
    },
    "heredoc_detection": {
      "tier1_triggered": false
    },
    "pack_evaluation": {
      "enabled_packs": ["core.git", "core.filesystem"],
      "matches": [
        {
          "pack_id": "core.git",
          "pattern_name": "reset-hard",
          "pattern": "git reset --hard",
          "reason": "Hard reset can permanently lose commits"
        }
      ]
    }
  },
  "decision": "deny",
  "rule_id": "core.git:reset-hard",
  "reason": "Hard reset can permanently lose commits",
  "suggestions": [
    {
      "kind": "preview_first",
      "text": "Run `git diff` first to see what would be lost"
    }
  ]
}
```

## Compact Output Format

```
DENY core.git:reset-hard "git reset --hard HEAD~5" -> Hard reset can permanently lose commits
```

## Implementation Notes

- Reuse the shared evaluator from 99e.3.1
- Add tracing/instrumentation hooks to capture each step
- Store trace in a `DecisionTrace` struct as evaluation proceeds
- Format the trace based on `--format` flag

## Performance

Explain mode is NOT on the hot path (user explicitly requests it), so:
- Can be slower than normal evaluation
- Can allocate for trace storage
- Should still complete in <100ms for typical commands

## Acceptance Criteria

- `dcg explain` works for all command types (simple, heredoc, pipes)
- Pretty format is human-readable and helps users understand decisions
- JSON format is valid and parseable by external tools
- Compact format is suitable for logging/scripting
- Suggestions are included in the output","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:23:51.003342694-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-07T21:55:30.329440887-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-07T21:55:30.329440887-05:00","close_reason":"Duplicate of git_safety_guard-1gt.1 + subtasks; superseded and split for better dependency tracking.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.6","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:23:51.004688188-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.7","title":"Improve dcg doctor (installation + config + hook health)","description":"## Objective

Make `dcg doctor` a *high-trust* diagnostic that rapidly answers:

- "Is dcg installed correctly and on PATH?"
- "Is the Claude Code hook registered correctly (exact matcher/command path)?"
- "Is configuration loading what I think it is (and is it valid)?"
- "If something is wrong, what exact command fixes it?"

This is an adoption lever: a guard that is hard to diagnose gets disabled.

## Current state (baseline)

`dcg doctor` exists (see `src/cli.rs`) and already performs basic checks, but it does not yet validate many real-world failure modes:

- wrong hook wiring (wrong tool matcher, wrong command, multiple hooks)
- config parse errors / unknown pack IDs
- cross-platform settings path nuances
- "it works in CLI but not in hook" mismatch scenarios

## Proposed doctor contract

### Output

- Human-readable by default.
- Optional machine-readable mode (JSON) for CI/automation/support.

### Checks (v1)

1) **Binary presence + version**
- Verify `dcg` resolves via PATH.
- Print version + build metadata.

2) **Claude Code settings discovery**
- Locate settings file reliably across platforms.
- If missing: warn-only (Claude Code may not be configured yet).

3) **Hook registration validation**
- Verify `hooks.PreToolUse` contains a `matcher: Bash` entry with a dcg hook.
- Detect duplicates and ambiguous entries.
- Validate that the configured hook command points to an executable (especially if absolute).

4) **Config discovery + parse + validation**
- Locate `~/.config/dcg/config.toml` (and any future supported locations).
- If present, parse and validate:
  - unknown pack IDs
  - conflicting enable/disable
  - overrides with invalid regex (once overrides are precompiled)

5) **Self-check smoke test (optional)**
- Prove the evaluator works end-to-end:
  - allow case (e.g., `git status`)
  - deny case (e.g., `git reset --hard`)

### Fix mode (`--fix`)

- Safe, explicit, non-destructive fixes only:
  - install hook if missing
  - create a sample config if missing (refuse to overwrite unless explicitly forced)
- Never delete or overwrite user files.

### Post-allowlist extension

Once allowlists ship, doctor should additionally validate allowlist presence/validity. This is tracked separately in `git_safety_guard-1gt.7.4` so the rest of doctor can ship earlier.

## Acceptance criteria

- Doctor catches and explains the top 5 real install/config/hook wiring failure modes.
- `dcg doctor --fix` is idempotent and performs only safe changes.
- Output is actionable (copy/paste next step).
- Tests cover settings parsing + hook detection on representative fixtures.

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-du4","title":"Implement language detection from command context","description":"# Implement Language Detection from Command Context

## Objective

Implement the language detection logic that determines which parser/patterns to use for heredoc content.

## Background

Based on the design (`git_safety_guard-jfj`), implement the multi-source language detection:

1. Command prefix lookup
2. Shebang detection
3. Content heuristics (fallback)

## Implementation Details

### Language Enum

```rust
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]
pub enum Language {
    Python,
    Bash,
    JavaScript,
    TypeScript,
    Ruby,
    Perl,
    Php,
    Unknown,
}

impl Language {
    /// Get the tree-sitter/ast-grep language identifier
    pub fn grammar_name(&self) -> Option<&'static str> {
        match self {
            Self::Python => Some("python"),
            Self::Bash => Some("bash"),
            Self::JavaScript => Some("javascript"),
            Self::TypeScript => Some("typescript"),
            Self::Ruby => Some("ruby"),
            Self::Perl => Some("perl"),
            Self::Php => Some("php"),
            Self::Unknown => None,
        }
    }
}
```

### Command Prefix Mapping

```rust
static COMMAND_LANGUAGE_MAP: LazyLock<HashMap<&'static str, Language>> = LazyLock::new(|| {
    let mut m = HashMap::new();
    // Python
    m.insert("python", Language::Python);
    m.insert("python3", Language::Python);
    m.insert("python2", Language::Python);
    // Bash/Shell
    m.insert("bash", Language::Bash);
    m.insert("sh", Language::Bash);
    m.insert("zsh", Language::Bash);
    m.insert("ksh", Language::Bash);
    // JavaScript/Node
    m.insert("node", Language::JavaScript);
    m.insert("nodejs", Language::JavaScript);
    // ... etc
    m
});

pub fn detect_from_command(cmd: &str) -> Option<Language> {
    // Extract first word (command name)
    let first_word = cmd.split_whitespace().next()?;
    // Handle paths: /usr/bin/python3 -> python3
    let basename = first_word.rsplit('/').next()?;
    COMMAND_LANGUAGE_MAP.get(basename).copied()
}
```

### Shebang Detection

```rust
pub fn detect_from_shebang(content: &str) -> Option<Language> {
    let first_line = content.lines().next()?;
    if !first_line.starts_with("#!") {
        return None;
    }
    // Parse: #!/usr/bin/env python3 -> python3
    // Parse: #!/bin/bash -> bash
    let interpreter = first_line
        .strip_prefix("#!")?
        .trim()
        .split_whitespace()
        .last()?
        .rsplit('/')
        .next()?;
    COMMAND_LANGUAGE_MAP.get(interpreter).copied()
}
```

### Content Heuristics (Fallback)

```rust
pub fn detect_from_content(content: &str) -> Option<Language> {
    // Very basic heuristics - only if nothing else works
    let lines: Vec<&str> = content.lines().take(10).collect();

    // Python indicators
    if lines.iter().any(|l| l.starts_with("import ") || l.starts_with("from ")) {
        return Some(Language::Python);
    }

    // JavaScript/Node indicators
    if lines.iter().any(|l| l.contains("require(") || l.contains("const ") || l.contains("let ")) {
        return Some(Language::JavaScript);
    }

    // Ruby indicators
    if lines.iter().any(|l| l.starts_with("def ") && content.contains("end")) {
        return Some(Language::Ruby);
    }

    None
}
```

### Combined Detection

```rust
pub fn detect_language(cmd: &str, heredoc: &HeredocContent) -> Language {
    // Priority 1: Command prefix
    if let Some(lang) = detect_from_command(cmd) {
        return lang;
    }

    // Priority 2: Shebang
    if let Some(lang) = detect_from_shebang(&heredoc.content) {
        return lang;
    }

    // Priority 3: Content heuristics
    if let Some(lang) = detect_from_content(&heredoc.content) {
        return lang;
    }

    Language::Unknown
}
```

## Testing

- Test command prefix for all supported commands
- Test shebang detection with various formats
- Test content heuristics
- Test fallback chain

## Acceptance Criteria

- Implements the priority order defined in `git_safety_guard-jfj` deterministically.
- Correctly identifies language for the majority of representative fixtures and returns `Unknown` for genuinely unknown cases.
- No panics on malformed input; bounded heuristics (only small prefix of content inspected).
- Provides enough diagnostics for `dcg explain` to show *why* a language was chosen (or why it was unknown).

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-e2eh","title":"Extend e2e_test.sh with heredoc test cases","description":"# E2E Test Script Extension for Heredocs

## Objective

Extend the existing `scripts/e2e_test.sh` with comprehensive heredoc test cases.

## Test Categories to Add

### 1. Heredoc Detection Tests

```bash
# Should BLOCK - Python heredoc with os.system
test_blocked 'python3 << EOF
import os
os.system("rm -rf /tmp/test")
EOF' "Python heredoc with os.system"

# Should BLOCK - Bash heredoc with rm
test_blocked 'bash << EOF
rm -rf /important
EOF' "Bash heredoc with rm -rf"

# Should BLOCK - Node inline script
test_blocked 'node -e "require(\x27child_process\x27).execSync(\x27rm -rf /\x27)"' "Node inline with execSync"
```

### 2. Heredoc Variant Tests

```bash
# Test all heredoc syntaxes
test_blocked 'cat <<EOF | python3
import os; os.system("rm -rf /")
EOF' "Piped heredoc"

test_blocked 'python3 <<-EOF
    import os
    os.system("rm -rf /")
EOF' "Indented heredoc (<<-)"

test_blocked 'python3 <<\x27EOF\x27
import os
os.system("rm -rf /")
EOF' "Quoted delimiter heredoc"
```

### 3. Language Coverage Tests

```bash
# Python
test_blocked 'python3 -c "import shutil; shutil.rmtree(\x27/tmp\x27)"'

# Ruby  
test_blocked 'ruby -e "FileUtils.rm_rf(\x27/tmp\x27)"'

# Perl
test_blocked 'perl -e "system(\x27rm -rf /\x27)"'

# JavaScript
test_blocked 'node -e "require(\x27fs\x27).rmdirSync(\x27/\x27, {recursive:true})"'
```

### 4. False Positive Tests

```bash
# Should ALLOW - Documentation with dangerous strings
test_allowed 'bd create --description="This pattern blocks rm -rf"' "Doc with rm -rf"
test_allowed 'git commit -m "Fix git reset --hard detection"' "Commit msg"
test_allowed 'grep "rm -rf" src/main.rs' "Grep for pattern"
test_allowed 'echo "example: git push --force"' "Echo example"
```

### 5. Bypass Attempt Tests

```bash
# Variable indirection
test_blocked 'bash -c "CMD=rm; $CMD -rf /"' "Variable indirection"

# Base64 encoding
test_blocked 'bash -c "echo cm0gLXJmIC8K | base64 -d | bash"' "Base64 bypass"

# String concatenation  
test_blocked 'python3 -c "exec(\x27r\x27+\x27m\x27+\x27 -rf /\x27)"' "String concat"
```

### 6. Edge Cases

```bash
# Empty heredoc
test_allowed 'cat << EOF
EOF' "Empty heredoc"

# Safe heredoc content
test_allowed 'python3 << EOF
print("hello world")
EOF' "Safe Python heredoc"

# Nested quotes
test_blocked 'bash -c "bash -c \x27rm -rf /\x27"' "Nested shells"
```

## Logging Enhancement

Add verbose mode:
```bash
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail

VERBOSE=${VERBOSE:-0}
LOG_FILE="e2e_test_$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S).log"

log() {
    echo "[$(date '+%H:%M:%S')] $*" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
}

test_blocked() {
    local cmd="$1"
    local desc="$2"
    log "TEST: $desc"
    log "  CMD: ${cmd:0:80}..."

    if echo "{\"tool_name\":\"Bash\",\"tool_input\":{\"command\":\"$cmd\"}}" | ./target/release/dcg 2>/dev/null; then
        log "  FAIL: Expected block, got allow"
        return 1
    else
        log "  PASS: Correctly blocked"
        return 0
    fi
}
```

## Output Summary

At end of test run:
```
=====================================
E2E Test Summary
=====================================
Total tests: 150
Passed: 148
Failed: 2
Skipped: 0

Failed tests:
  - Python heredoc with subprocess (line 234)
  - Node inline with dynamic require (line 456)

Log file: e2e_test_20260107_193045.log
=====================================
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- `scripts/e2e_test.sh` contains a dedicated heredoc section with:
  - true-positive blocks across at least 3 languages
  - false-positive regression cases (doc/test strings)
  - a small set of bypass-attempt cases
- Verbose logging is available and includes per-test:
  - test name
  - normalized command (truncated)
  - decision (allow/deny) and rule attribution when denied
  - failure diagnostics sufficient for CI triage
- The heredoc E2E suite runs deterministically and completes in a reasonable time budget (define target after baseline).

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-e7m","title":"Integrate heredoc detection into main check pipeline","description":"## Objective

Integrate all heredoc detection components into the main dcg check pipeline, creating a seamless flow from command input to block/allow decision.

## Pipeline Architecture

```
JSON Input → Parse → Quick Reject → Normalize →
  → Heredoc Detection → [if heredoc found] →
    → Extract Content → Detect Language → Parse with ast-grep →
    → Apply Language Patterns → Block Decision
  → [no heredoc] → Existing Pattern Matching → Block Decision
```

## Integration Points

### 1. Entry Point Hook

Modify main.rs check flow to:

1. After quick reject passes, check for heredoc indicators
2. If heredoc found, branch to heredoc analysis path
3. If no heredoc, continue with existing pattern matching
4. Merge results from both paths

### 2. Heredoc Detection Integration

Insert heredoc detection before main pattern matching:

```rust
fn check_command(cmd: &str) -> CheckResult {
    // Quick reject (existing)
    if global_quick_reject(cmd) {
        return CheckResult::allowed();
    }

    // NEW: Heredoc detection
    if let Some(heredoc_result) = check_heredoc(cmd) {
        if heredoc_result.blocked {
            return heredoc_result;
        }
    }

    // Existing pattern matching
    REGISTRY.check_command(cmd, &enabled_packs)
}
```

### 3. Performance Budget

Total additional latency budget must be bounded.

- Tier 1 trigger: microseconds
- Tier 2 extraction: ~1ms typical
- Tier 3 AST/pattern: bounded by timeout (hard cap)

If AST analysis is too slow, fail-open (allow) in hook mode and emit a diagnostic.

### 4. Feature Flag / Pack integration

Add heredoc scanning to pack system:

- New pack: "heredoc" (or integrate into core)
- Can be enabled/disabled via configuration
- Default policy should be conservative (warn-first / strict mode opt-in)

### 5. Error Handling

Graceful degradation:

- AST engine unavailable → allow (hook) + diagnostic; optional fallback to simpler scanning
- AST timeout → allow (hook) + diagnostic
- Parse error → allow (hook) + diagnostic
- Unknown language → allow (hook) or generic high-signal checks only

## Configuration

New config options:

```toml
[heredoc]
enabled = true
timeout_ms = 50
fallback_on_error = true
languages = ["python", "bash", "javascript", "typescript", "ruby", "perl"]
```

## Testing Integration

Integration tests should cover:

- Normal commands (no heredoc) still work fast
- Heredoc commands get analyzed
- Pattern matches in heredocs block correctly
- Safe heredocs pass through
- Error paths handle gracefully

## Acceptance Criteria

- Heredoc path is executed only when Tier 1 triggers; non-heredoc commands remain fast.
- In hook mode, any heredoc parsing timeout/error fails open (allow) with a trace/log marker.
- At least one E2E test demonstrates a real heredoc block (direct destructive payload).
- At least one E2E test demonstrates a false-positive suppression case (dangerous string in data-only context).
- Decisions are attributed with stable rule IDs (pack_id + pattern_name) so explain/allowlist workflows are possible.

-{"id":"git_safety_guard-ehc","title":"Implement GitLab CI extractor (.gitlab-ci.yml)","description":"## Objective

Implement an extractor for GitLab CI YAML files to detect executable command contexts.

## File Pattern

- `.gitlab-ci.yml`
- `*.gitlab-ci.yml` (includes)

## Extraction Targets

### Script Blocks (executed)
- `script:` arrays
- `before_script:` arrays
- `after_script:` arrays

### NOT Extracted (data only)
- `variables:` values
- `rules:` conditions
- `only:`/`except:` patterns
- Comments

## Example

```yaml
deploy:
  script:
    - kubectl delete namespace prod  # EXTRACT THIS
  variables:
    DANGEROUS: "rm -rf /"  # DO NOT extract
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- Correctly extracts script blocks with line numbers
- Ignores variable definitions and comments
- Handles YAML anchors and aliases
- Unit tests with representative fixtures","notes":"## Review Additions (2026-01-08)

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-erux","title":"Design user-friendly error messages for heredoc blocks","description":"# User-Friendly Error Messages for Heredoc Blocking

## Objective

When a heredoc command is blocked, provide clear, actionable error messages that help users understand WHY it was blocked and HOW to proceed.

## Current Error Format (for reference)

```
╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
║  ⚠️  BLOCKED: Destructive Command Detected                   ║
╠══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣
║  Command: git reset --hard HEAD~5                            ║
║  Reason:  Hard reset can permanently lose commits            ║
║  Pack:    core.git                                           ║
╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝
```

## Enhanced Error Format for Heredocs

```
╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
║  ⚠️  BLOCKED: Destructive Pattern in Heredoc                 ║
╠══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣
║  Language: Python                                            ║
║  Pattern:  os.system($CMD)                                   ║
║  Matched:  os.system("rm -rf /tmp/data")                    ║
║  Line:     3 of heredoc                                      ║
║  Reason:   Shell command execution via os.system             ║
║  Pack:     heredoc.python                                    ║
╠══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣
║  Context:                                                    ║
║    1│ import os                                              ║
║    2│ path = "/tmp/data"                                    ║
║  ▶ 3│ os.system("rm -rf /tmp/data")  ← BLOCKED             ║
║    4│ print("done")                                         ║
╠══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣
║  💡 Suggestions:                                             ║
║  • Use pathlib.Path.unlink() for single file deletion       ║
║  • Use shutil.rmtree() with explicit path validation        ║
║  • If intentional, use dcg allow --reason "..."             ║
╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝
```

## Error Message Components

### 1. Header

- Clear indication this is a BLOCK, not just a warning
- Specify it's a heredoc/inline script detection

### 2. Detection Details

- Language detected
- Pattern name that matched
- Actual matched text (truncated if long)
- Line number within heredoc
- Human-readable reason

### 3. Context Window

- Show 2-3 lines before and after the match
- Highlight the offending line with arrow
- Syntax highlighting if terminal supports it

### 4. Suggestions

- Language-specific safe alternatives
- How to override if intentional
- Link to documentation

## JSON Output Format

For Claude Code hook protocol:

```json
{
  "hookSpecificOutput": {
    "permissionDecision": "deny",
    "reason": "Heredoc contains destructive pattern: os.system with shell command",
    "details": {
      "detection_type": "heredoc",
      "language": "python",
      "pattern": "os.system($CMD)",
      "matched_text": "os.system(\"rm -rf /tmp/data\")",
      "line_in_heredoc": 3,
      "pack": "heredoc.python",
      "suggestions": [
        "Use pathlib.Path.unlink() for single file deletion",
        "Use shutil.rmtree() with explicit path validation"
      ]
    }
  }
}
```

## Suggestions Database

Maintain per-pattern suggestions:

```rust
static PATTERN_SUGGESTIONS: &[(&str, &[&str])] = &[
    ("os.system", &[
        "Use subprocess.run() with shell=False",
        "Use pathlib for file operations",
    ]),
    ("shutil.rmtree", &[
        "Validate path is within expected directory",
        "Use explicit allowlist of deletable paths",
    ]),
];
```

## Test Cases

- Verify all pattern types have suggestions
- Verify context window is correctly extracted
- Verify line numbers are accurate
- Verify JSON output is valid
- Verify color output is readable
- Verify truncation for long matches

## Acceptance Criteria

- When heredoc detection blocks, stderr output includes:
  - language
  - stable rule ID (pack_id:pattern_name)
  - a small context window with highlighted line
  - actionable next steps (explain/allowlist)
- Hook JSON denial remains valid and includes enough structured details for tooling.
- Output is safe for non-TTY environments (no ANSI when not a TTY).
- Unit tests cover at least:
  - context window extraction
  - truncation
  - JSON schema validity

Improve E2E scripts to produce **actionable, structured logs** and timing data for failures.

## Scope

- Add per-test timing and aggregate timing summary.
- Optional JSON log output (machine-readable), alongside current human-friendly logs.
- Capture stdout/stderr on failure and store as artifacts in CI.

## Acceptance criteria

- Failures show expected vs actual plus captured output.
- Verbose mode includes timings and test IDs.
- CI artifacts include log files for failed runs.

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-f4f","title":"Implement ast-grep-core pattern matching layer","description":"# ast-grep-core Pattern Matching Layer

## Objective

Integrate ast-grep-core as a Rust library for structural pattern matching in heredoc/inline-script content.

## Architecture decision

Prefer **embedded ast-grep-core** over shelling out to a CLI, because:

- avoids per-invocation process spawn overhead
- keeps a single-binary distribution story
- enables richer match metadata (spans) for `dcg explain`

(Still subject to ADR confirmation; this task implements whichever embedded approach is chosen.)

## Dependencies to add (tentative)

```toml
[dependencies]
ast-grep-core = "0.40"
ast-grep-language = { version = "0.37", features = [
    "bash",
    "python",
    "javascript",
    "typescript",
    "ruby"
    # Note: Perl may not be available
] }
```

## API design

```rust
/// Pattern matcher using ast-grep-core
pub struct AstMatcher {
    patterns: HashMap<Language, Vec<CompiledPattern>>,
}

impl AstMatcher {
    /// Create matcher with default destructive patterns
    pub fn new() -> Self;

    /// Load patterns from config / registry
    pub fn from_config(config: &PatternConfig) -> Result<Self>;

    /// Match patterns against code (bounded)
    pub fn find_matches(&self, code: &str, language: Language) -> Result<Vec<PatternMatch>, MatchError>;
}

/// Result of pattern matching
pub struct PatternMatch {
    pub rule_id: String,             // stable: pack_id:pattern_name
    pub reason: String,
    pub matched_text_preview: String,
    pub start: usize,                // byte offset (or row/col)
    pub end: usize,
    pub line_number: usize,
    pub severity: Severity,          // ties into decision policy
}

pub enum Severity { Error, Warning, Info }
```

## Pattern definition format (example)

Patterns should be defined and reviewed under `git_safety_guard-6sg` (pattern library structure). The matching layer must be agnostic to *what* the patterns are, but must support:

- stable IDs
- per-pattern severity
- per-pattern suggestions (optional)

## Error handling / fail-open

In hook mode:

- Unsupported language → skip analysis (allow) + trace marker
- Parse error → skip analysis (allow) + trace marker
- Timeout → skip analysis (allow) + trace marker

In `dcg test` / `dcg explain` / `dcg scan`:

- return structured diagnostics (not silent skip)

## Performance targets (initial)

- Pattern compilation: one-time at startup (bounded)
- Parse: <2ms for typical heredoc sizes
- Match: <1ms typical
- Total AST path: bounded by a hard timeout

## Acceptance Criteria

- Implements an embedded AST matcher that can:
  - parse at least one supported language
  - match at least one simple pattern
  - return match spans + line number
- Enforces a hard timeout for AST work and fails open in hook mode on timeout.
- Produces match metadata compatible with:
  - `dcg explain` highlighting (matched span + preview)
  - rule attribution (stable rule_id)
  - decision policy (severity)
- Includes unit tests for:
  - a positive match
  - a negative (safe) snippet
  - an unsupported-language path
  - a timeout path (if testable)
","notes":"## Review Additions (2026-01-08)

### False positive control
This layer must support the FP-immunity strategy, not undermine it:

- matchers should attach stable rule IDs + severity so policy can default-warn vs deny
- matchers should support negative fixtures / “must-not-match” tests per rule family
- prefer composite/high-signal rules (AST match + literal destructive payload) over broad matches (e.g., `exec($VAR)`)

### Tests
- For each supported language: at least one positive and one negative snippet.
- Span/line mapping test: matches return accurate location for explain output.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-07T18:32:24.313325332-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-08T02:41:21.25882879-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-08T02:41:21.25882879-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented ast-grep-core pattern matching layer with AstMatcher struct, find_matches(), timeout handling, and 17 unit tests. All acceptance criteria met.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-f4f","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-5ib","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T18:32:32.909572665-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-f4f","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-du4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T18:32:32.929896409-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-f4f","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-yza","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:50:18.965756128-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
